table_id: <file_alias>.image                   // stable id, one card per image, e.g. I1.image
title: <human title — what the image is (from its content / filename)>

source.as_of: <image vintage — ALWAYS set; dates every answer built on the image>
source.as_of_basis: <stated | inferred | unknown — how the vintage was determined>
source.captured_date: <optional: capture time from EXIF/metadata, if present>

summary: <3-6 plain sentences: what the image shows and what it is FOR. Written after a DIRECT visual read of the image, not just OCR. Derivable only from the image.>
transcript: <the text visible in the image, verbatim-ish (OCR cross-checked by a visual read). This is the grounded artifact query.py returns. For a chart, transcribe axis labels, series, and any printed values.>
use_cases: <a real question this image is the right source for>
<another — 'what does the chart show for X', 'who/what is depicted', 'what value does it give for Y'>

entities.name: <canonical entity name (normalize company/person names via aliases.csv)>
entities.why: <short: why this entity appears in the image / its role>

content_dates.value: <a date the image's data refers to, e.g. '2024' or 'Q3 2025'>
content_dates.basis: <stated (printed on the image / in metadata) | inferred (from context)>
content_dates.note: <what datapoint this date attaches to, e.g. 'x-axis 2020-2024' or 'footer as-of'>

not_answerable.q: <a plausible question this image CANNOT answer>
